questions.question_text: Idaho real estate license renewal cycles are based on what?
questions.explanation: Idaho uses a 2-year renewal cycle that starts on the first day of the month following the licensee's birth month. This staggers renewals throughout the year rather than creating a single deadline.

questions.question_text: How long is an Idaho real estate license renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: Idaho licenses are renewed on a 2-year cycle. The cycle begins on the first day of the month following the licensee's birth month.

questions.question_text: A salesperson practices real estate after their license has lapsed. What violation has occurred?
questions.explanation: Practicing real estate after a license lapses is practicing without an active license, which is a serious violation subject to disciplinary action. The licensee must complete all renewal requirements before resuming practice.

questions.question_text: A licensee fails to renew their license by the deadline. What happens to their license status?
distractors: It remains active with a late fee
questions.explanation: Failure to renew by the deadline causes the license to lapse to inactive status. Inactive licensees cannot perform any licensed real estate activities until they complete renewal requirements.

questions.question_text: How many Commission Core courses must an Idaho licensee complete during each renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: Idaho requires 2 Commission Core courses per renewal cycle — one each year. Each Core course can be up to 4 hours. These are in addition to 12 hours of elective CE.

questions.question_text: In addition to Commission Core courses, how many hours of elective CE must an Idaho licensee complete per renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: Idaho licensees must complete 12 hours of elective CE in addition to the 2 Commission Core courses per renewal cycle. The total CE obligation includes Core courses plus 12 elective hours.

questions.question_text: A licensee completes both Core courses but only 8 hours of elective CE before renewal. What is the consequence?
questions.correct_answer: License lapses to inactive until CE is completed
distractors: License renews with a late fee
The missing 4 hours carry to next cycle
questions.explanation: All CE requirements must be completed before renewal. Failing to complete the required 12 elective hours (only 8 were done) means the license lapses to inactive status until the requirement is fulfilled.

questions.question_text: What is the maximum length of each Commission Core course?
questions.explanation: Each Commission Core course can be up to 4 hours in length. Licensees must complete one Core course each year of the 2-year renewal cycle.

questions.question_text: Which combination correctly describes Idaho's total CE requirements per renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: Idaho requires 2 Commission Core courses (one per year, up to 4 hours each) plus 12 hours of elective continuing education per 2-year renewal cycle.

questions.question_text: A newly licensed salesperson is approaching their first renewal. What additional education is required beyond standard CE?
distractors: No additional requirements beyond standard CE
questions.explanation: First-time renewing salespersons must complete 12 hours of Post License Education, which must include the 8-hour Post License Fundamentals course. This is in addition to the 2 Core courses.

questions.question_text: The Post License Education requirement for a first-renewal salesperson must include which specific course?
questions.explanation: The 12 hours of Post License Education required at first renewal must include the 8-hour Post License Fundamentals course. The remaining 4 hours can be other approved post-license courses.

questions.question_text: What happens to a licensee whose license has lapsed to inactive status?
questions.correct_answer: They cannot perform any licensed activities
distractors: They can still close pending transactions
They can work under direct broker supervision
They have 60 days to complete requirements
questions.explanation: An inactive licensee cannot perform any licensed real estate activities. There is no grace period for pending transactions. The licensee must complete all renewal requirements to reactivate.

questions.question_text: For their first renewal, an Idaho salesperson must complete how many total hours of post-license education?
questions.explanation: First-renewal salespersons must complete 12 hours of Post License Education. This includes the mandatory 8-hour Post License Fundamentals course plus 4 additional hours of approved post-license education.

questions.question_text: A salesperson on their second renewal must complete which of the following?
questions.correct_answer: 2 Core courses plus 12 elective CE hours
questions.explanation: After the first renewal, standard CE requirements apply: 2 Commission Core courses (one per year) plus 12 hours of elective CE. Post-license education is only required at first renewal. The Brokerage Management course is for broker candidates.
